title: sub_GAME_7F03ECC0 bbox-hull vertex count clamped to 4 in port (retail writes up to 6-8 hull vertices, no clamp)
repro: Collision-bounds path for a prop/door whose screen-projected axis-aligned bounding box (linear 3D-to-2D map using matrix X and Z columns) forms a pentagon or hexagon convex hull -- e.g. an angled/rotated prop viewed obliquely. Retail collision polygon has 5-6 vertices; port has at most 4. No runtime flag toggles this.

history.note: Retail sub_GAME_7F03ECC0 (glabel chrprop.c:8243, US) projects the 8 bbox corners to 2D, finds the four extreme corners (min-x/tiebreak-min-y, max-y/tiebreak-min-x, max-x/tiebreak-max-y, min-y/tiebreak-max-x -- all four search loops verified with correct comparison senses), then for each of the four hull edges inserts at most one remaining vertex using the cross-product test px*edge_dy < py*edge_dx (all four edge loops verified). Output vertex total is thus up to 8 (4 corners + up to 4 inserts); for a box projected by a linear map the hull is a hexagon at most, so 5 or 6 is reachable. RETAIL TAIL (chrprop.c:8710-8712): loads data ptr, blez count skip, then sw count into data unk00 with NO clamp; the compiler-unrolled translate loop (andi count and 3 remainder + groups of 4) adds matrix row-3 components at plus 0x30 and plus 0x38 to ALL count output vertices. Live C (chrprop.c:8229) inserts 'if (count > 4) count = 4;' BEFORE storing data unk00 and before the translate loop. Callers (chrobjhandler.c:2927, 11890, 45882) pass rect = address of collision_data unk04 and data = collision_data, so the count field and the polygon buffer are contiguous; rect4f (bondtypes.h:361) is a union of 4 coord2d, so retail writing 5-8 vertices continues past unk04 into the trailing collision_data words (unk24 onward). DIVERGENCE: when the projected-hull has more than 4 vertices, the port stores count=4 and emits a 4-vertex collision polygon, whereas retail stores the true count (5-6) and emits that many vertices (writing the extra ones into the words following rect4f). Consumers read data unk00 as the polygon vertex count, so the port produces a smaller collision hull for such props. The C comment claims 'Original N64 bug; matches original behavior' but retail performs no clamp -- the claim is incorrect. Classified parity-divergence: the clamp is a deliberate port change (avoids retail's write past the 4-vertex rect4f) but it lacks the required A/B negative-control flag and faithful-mode decision. Faithful-mode resolution deferred pending confirmation of whether the trailing collision_data words are intended extra polygon slots (retail self-consistent, port under-computes) or independent fields (retail overrun is the n64-quirk). Everything else in the function verified equivalent (8 vertex projections, four extreme searches, four cross-product edge inserts, remaining-vertex collector, matrix translate). Found by ASM-audit batch 10, queue rank 106, region US.
Batch-10 rank 106 hull vertex clamp; adjudicating fix-vs-document.

Retail sub_GAME_7F03ECC0 (ASM chrprop.c:8710-8712) stores the true hull count (up to 8, geometrically <=6) with NO clamp; the port's 'if(count>4)count=4' + the 'Original N64 bug' claim are wrong. ADJUDICATION: the collision_data caller path (chrobjhandler.c:2927/45882) writes &collision_data->unk04 followed by spare words unk24..unk40 that chrpropTestPointInPolygon reads contiguously (safe to fill). BUT the TANK caller (chrobjhandler.c:11890) writes TankRecord.rect (bondtypes.h:3719) immediately followed by live fields (unkA4 acceleration, unkA8...) in the port's 64-bit-grown struct, and the tank consumer (bondview.c:8881) reads unk80->unk00 vertices from &tank->rect. So >4 vertices there corrupt/misread tank state. The clamp is a load-bearing port safety mitigation for the tank path.

Faithful-mode decision: KEEP the clamp to 4 as the memory-safe default (tank path has no spare slots in the port layout). GE007_HULL_VERTS_RETAIL (opt-in, default OFF) removes the clamp — retail-faithful for the object/door path, NOT tank-safe. Factored hullVertexCount(count, retail_unclamped); default path byte-identical to the pre-fix clamp. ROM-free ctest hull_vertex_clamp guards both modes.
